perturbation.original_text: 1.1 The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and terminate upon the conclusion of the week following the conclusion of the Dolphins 2000 regular season or post season, if applicable (the "Term" or "Initial Term"). However, this Agreement may be earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and terminate upon the conclusion of the week following the conclusion of the Dolphins 2000 regular season or post season, if applicable (the "Term" or "Initial Term"). However, this Agreement may be earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof. Notwithstanding the above, for the purposes of Section 3.1, the Term shall be considered to end on December 31, 1999.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the 'Term' of the agreement. Section 1.1 defines the Term ending after the 2000 season, but the added sentence states that for Section 3.1 (Consideration), the Term ends on December 31, 1999. This creates an in-text contradiction. In Section 3.1 it is not clear for how long Hydron should pay the Dolphins the sum of $24,000, considering the Term now has conflicting dates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 In consideration of the Sponsorship Rights granted to Hydron hereunder: (a) Hydron shall pay to the Dolphins an aggregate amount of $96,000 (plus any applicable sales and other taxes) as follows: Hydron shall pay Dolphins the sum of $24,000 (plus any applicable sales and other taxes) on July 1, 1997, July 1, 1998, July 1, 1999 and July 1, 2000.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by defining the 'Term' of the agreement differently in Section 1.1 and for the purposes of Section 3.1. Section 1.1 states the Term ends after the 2000 season, while the added sentence states that for Section 3.1, the Term ends on December 31, 1999. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which outlines payment obligations based on the 'Sponsorship Rights granted to Hydron hereunder'. Because the term has two definitions, Hydron's payment obligations described in Section 3.1 are now ambiguous and contradictory, making it unclear for which period Hydron must pay the Dolphins as part of the Term of the Agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 All advertising material and any use of the other parties' Marks by a party is subject to the prior written approval of the Mark owner. Either party shall submit all such materials or proposed usage of a Mark to the other party at least two weeks prior to its intended use. The Mark owner shall have the right to inspect and require changes or deletions (including the right to disapprove of such advertisement or use of Marks in their entirety) of advertising and promotional copy or material that the Mark owner may deem to be contrary to its policies or best interests. Such requirements will not be unreasonably imposed, and the foregoing approvals and 2 requirements will be consistently given and imposed on all sponsors or users of the Marks, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 All advertising material and any use of the other parties' Marks by a party is subject to the prior written approval of the Mark owner. Either party shall submit all such materials or proposed usage of a Mark to the other party at least two weeks prior to its intended use. The Mark owner shall have complete discretion to approve or disapprove advertising and promotional copy or material. Such approvals and requirements will be consistently given and imposed on all sponsors or users of the Marks, as the case may be.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase stating that requirements will not be unreasonably imposed, and adding that the Mark owner has 'complete discretion', Section 4.2 now contradicts itself. It was initially stated that approval processes would be reasonable and now approval is subject to complete discretion. This introduces an in-text contradiction within section 4.2 and makes the application of the Mark usage inconsistent and unpredictable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 All advertising material and any use of the other parties' Marks by a party is subject to the prior written approval of the Mark owner. Either party shall submit all such materials or proposed usage of a Mark to the other party at least two weeks prior to its intended use. The Mark owner shall have complete discretion to approve or disapprove advertising and promotional copy or material. Such approvals and requirements will be consistently given and imposed on all sponsors or users of the Marks, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction within Section 4.2. Originally, the Mark owner's requirements were to be reasonable. The changed text grants the Mark owner 'complete discretion,' negating the reasonableness requirement. This creates an in-text contradiction because the clause now simultaneously states that the Mark owner has complete freedom and that approvals/requirements will be consistently given, implying a reasonable standard, yet simultaneously removing the explicit requirement for reasonableness. This creates ambiguity and makes the application of the Mark usage inconsistent and unpredictable, directly contradicting the original intent as described in the Perturbation Explanation.
